title: Papaya enzyme face mask
subtitle: A fresh-use enzyme face mask made with blended ripe papaya and kaolin clay
excerpt: Ripe papaya contains papain, a protease enzyme that breaks down dead surface cells on the skin when applied as a mask. Combined with a small amount of kaolin clay to give the mask body and some absorbency, and honey for texture, this is a fresh-use preparation made in small amounts and used immediately.
sourceNotes: Papain as a skin enzyme: Starley et al., Journal of Wound Care (1999), papain-based debridement. Cosmetic use of papain in enzyme masks: Draelos and Thaman, Cosmetic Formulation of Skin Care Products (2006). Kaolin as a mask base: standard cosmetic formulation reference. This preparation contains no preservative and must be used fresh.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make immediately before use. This preparation has no preservative and contains fresh fruit; do not store.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use immediately after making. Discard any leftover preparation. Fresh papaya contains active enzymes that degrade within hours once the fruit is blended.

glossaryTerms.definition: An application of a small amount of a cosmetic preparation to the inner forearm or behind the ear, left for 24 to 48 hours, to check for a reaction before using on the face.

content.text: This mask uses the enzyme activity in fresh ripe papaya as its active component. Papain is a protease enzyme naturally present in papaya fruit that breaks down proteins on the skin surface when applied as a mask. The effect is a smoother surface after rinsing. Kaolin clay gives the mask a spreadable texture and some absorbency. Make a small amount immediately before use; this preparation has no preservative and does not keep.

attrs.title: Use ripe papaya
attrs.body: The enzyme activity is highest in fully ripe papaya. An unripe papaya has more latex, which can cause reactions in some people, and less ripe fruit means less enzyme activity.

items.prepNote: ripe; flesh only, no skin or seeds; roughly 2 tablespoons blended
1 teaspoon; gives the mask body and mild absorbency
1 teaspoon; for texture and skin-softening properties

content.text: Blend the papaya flesh until smooth. Stir in the kaolin clay and honey until all lumps are gone. The mask should be a thick, spreadable paste.

Apply to a clean, dry face, avoiding the eye area and lips. Leave for 10 to 15 minutes. The skin may feel slightly warm or tingly; this is normal. Remove with warm water and a damp cloth. Do not exceed 15 minutes and do not use if the skin is broken or sunburned.
Before first use, apply a small amount to the

area (inner forearm) and leave for 15 minutes. Rinse and wait 24 hours before using on the face. Papain is a protein enzyme and can cause a reaction in people with latex sensitivity or papaya allergy.

Use immediately after making. Discard any leftover preparation. Fresh papaya contains active enzymes that degrade quickly once blended, and the mask has no preservative. Do not store, refrigerate, or reuse.
